description: Kill dead main() functions and secure the hardcoded Telegram token. Remove main() from all orphaned entry points (main_system.cpp, main_plugins.cpp, main_network.cpp, ai_core/main.cpp, scheduler/main_scheduler.cpp, ai_core/hre/hre_main.cpp) by either deleting the file or converting it to a module-only file. Also move the hardcoded Telegram bot token from source code to environment variable loading.
steps: Delete MK_OS/system/main_system.cpp entirely - it is fully superseded by mk_entry.cpp and only contains a dead demo sequence
Delete MK_OS/plugins/main_plugins.cpp entirely - same reason, dead standalone demo that duplicates plugin initialization

Remove the int main() function from MK_OS/network/main_network.cpp. Keep the #includes and keep it as a module file that provides MKNetwork/MKHTTP/MKNetworkParser/MKDatasetUpdater classes (the #includes of mk_network.cpp, http.cpp, parser.cpp, update.cpp are useful). Wrap remaining content in an #ifndef guard if not already.

Remove the int main() function from MK_OS/ai_core/main.cpp. Keep it as a module that aggregates AI core includes (memory.cpp, reasoning.cpp, language.cpp, execution.cpp, feedback.cpp, coding.cpp, writing.cpp, nexus.cpp, datasets). Keep class instantiation logic but move it into a utility struct or namespace.
Remove the int main() function from MK_OS/scheduler/main_scheduler.cpp. Keep the includes of mk_scheduler.cpp and cpu.cpp. Convert to module-only.

Remove the int main() function from MK_OS/ai_core/hre/hre_main.cpp - the HRE REPL there is superseded by mk_entry.cpp's natural language handling. Keep any useful includes if they are unique (check if pattern_graph, reasoning_chains, query_parser, composer are already included by mk_entry.cpp).

Modify MK_OS/plugins/telegram.cpp: Add a static method or constructor overload that reads the token from the MK_TELEGRAM_TOKEN environment variable using std::getenv(). The constructor MKTelegram(const std::string& botToken) can stay, but add MKTelegram() default constructor that auto-loads from env var. Print a warning if the env var is not set.

Remove the hardcoded token string '8694681522:AAHQ0SNSAm-wMZ2enbbn-4cNF8vV3eBsPqc' from any remaining files (it was only in main_system.cpp and main_plugins.cpp which are being deleted, but verify no other occurrences).
Verify the build still compiles: clang++ -std=c++17 -O2 -Wall -Wextra -DDARWIN -o mk_os system/mk_entry.cpp -lcurl -lsqlite3
acceptance_criteria: No file in the project contains the string '8694681522' (the hardcoded token is gone)
MK_OS/network/main_network.cpp, MK_OS/ai_core/main.cpp, MK_OS/scheduler/main_scheduler.cpp, and MK_OS/ai_core/hre/hre_main.cpp exist but do NOT contain 'int main()'
MK_OS/plugins/telegram.cpp has a default constructor or factory that reads MK_TELEGRAM_TOKEN from environment
The project builds successfully with the build command
verification: Run: grep -rn 'int main()' /projects/sandbox/MK_os-2/MK_OS/ --include='*.cpp' | grep -v mk_entry | grep -v tools/ | grep -v Kernel/ (should return nothing or only code_intelligence.cpp string literals)
